comment: Move 0 hand-authored tree — canonical product/domain/service model. 7 Products, 33 Domains. Per chorus-product-tree.md (Jeff+Wren 2026-05-14). All fields populated: ownedBy, atStep, status, comment, gaps on every product and domain.

products.label: Loom
products.comment: Team knowledge layer — the values, roles, principles, policies, practices, and skills that define how the team operates.

products.label: Athena
products.comment: Structural model — the canonical product/domain/service ontology. Gates, SessionStart envelopes, and blast-radius routing all read from here.

products.label: Convergence
products.comment: Integration layer — connections to external systems, NiFi pipelines, and cross-product data flows.

products.label: Chorus
products.comment: Shared memory and knowledge for the team — indexed sessions, Slack history, and semantic search across all team artifacts.

products.label: The Clearing
products.comment: Interaction layer — messages, streams, the spine event log, and the kanban board. How the team talks and tracks work.

products.label: Werk
products.comment: Execution substrate — pipelines, version control, code quality, and test infrastructure. How cards become shipped software.

products.label: Borg
products.comment: Observability and operations — builds, deploys, logs, alerts, properties, security, and infrastructure health.

domains.label: roles
domains.comment: Team member identities, capabilities, and session lifecycle.

domains.label: principles
domains.comment: Values and constraints governing team decisions and priorities.

domains.label: policies
domains.comment: Rules that enforce principles in practice — hooks, guards, gates.
domains.consumesNote: context_inject reads crawler messages + spine + LanceDB on EVERY prompt; degraded grounding for all roles if it stops (#3071)

domains.label: practices
domains.comment: Repeatable patterns the team uses — how we work, not just what we value.

domains.label: skills
domains.comment: Invokable capabilities — /skill-name commands that automate team workflows.

domains.label: decisions
domains.comment: Decision log with context, rationale, and tradeoffs recorded.

domains.label: rcas
domains.comment: Root cause analyses — what broke, why, and what changed as a result.

domains.label: domains
domains.comment: The canonical product/domain/service structural model — this tree.

domains.label: services
domains.comment: Service definitions, interfaces, and ownership within the product model.

domains.label: integrations
domains.comment: Connections to external systems — NiFi, APIs, cross-product data flows.

domains.label: memory
domains.comment: Persistent context across sessions — indexed sessions and artifacts.
domains.consumesNote: indexSpine re-indexes crawler.* spine events into the index (#3071)

domains.label: knowledge
domains.comment: Structured facts about the team, products, and their history.
domains.dependsOnNote: crawler-hydrate-graph.sh writes chorus:File RDF triples; /crawl collectRdf reads them. Graph RDF bucket empties + stale-flag detection stops if it stops (#3071)

domains.label: search
domains.comment: Semantic retrieval across all indexed team knowledge and session history.
domains.consumesNote: FTS/hybrid reads crawler messages; semantic reads crawler LanceDB vectors. Loses domain layer + domain vectors if it stops (#3071)

domains.label: messages
domains.comment: Direct role-to-role communications — nudges and the bridge messaging API.

domains.label: streams
domains.comment: Event streams and pub/sub channels the team uses to coordinate.

domains.label: spine
domains.comment: The audit trail — structured log of all team activity and state changes.

domains.label: cards
domains.comment: Kanban board and card lifecycle — the unit of work tracking for the team.

domains.label: pipelines
domains.comment: CI/CD and build orchestration — how code moves from commit to production.

domains.label: version-control
domains.comment: Git workflow, branches, worktrees, hooks, and commit policy enforcement.

domains.label: code
domains.comment: Source code quality — linting, type-checking, and static analysis.

domains.label: tests
domains.comment: Automated test suites — jest, bats, cargo test, and integration tests.

domains.label: builds
domains.comment: Build artifacts, Rust binary signing, and cdhash provenance.

domains.label: deploys
domains.comment: Deployment records, rollback paths, and chorus-deploy orchestration.

domains.label: logs
domains.comment: Log aggregation and querying via Loki — structured observability.

domains.label: properties
domains.comment: Service configuration and runtime properties across Library and Bedroom.

domains.label: alerts-monitors
domains.comment: Grafana dashboards, alert rules, and on-call signal management.
domains.dependsOnNote: crawler-stale/error/domain-failure/hydration-divergence read crawler spine + /tmp status. crawler-stale's 5-min threshold will FALSE-FIRE under the #3068 30-min cadence — align threshold (#3071)

domains.label: security-trust
domains.comment: Authentication, authorization, secrets management, and TCC permissions.

domains.label: time
domains.comment: Scheduling, cron jobs, and time-based triggers for automated team work.

domains.label: heralds
domains.comment: Notification delivery — nudge routing, chorus-inject, and push delivery.

domains.label: toolchain
domains.comment: Development tools and their configuration — Claude Code, Node, Rust, git.

domains.label: infrastructure
domains.comment: Host machines, Docker, LaunchAgents — the physical substrate the team runs on.

domains.label: analytics
domains.comment: Usage analytics and behavioral signals — attention, interaction patterns.
domains.consumesNote: pain rollup reads crawler.* spine events for failure attribution (#3071)

domains.label: metrics
domains.comment: Quantitative measurements of system health — latency, cost, throughput.

services.label: crawler
services.comment: Monolithic snapshot crawler — index-crawler-snapshots.sh (11 collectors x 27 domains) + crawler-hydrate-graph.sh (RDF). VERIFIED dependency map via subagent traces against the running system (#3071), not inference. One monolith, four owners; pending decomposition into per-collector instances (#3069/ADR-033). Full map: designing/docs/crawler-dependency-map.html.

produces.note: 108 rows/min, 144K of 301K total, NO load-bearing consumer found — cut/sample candidate, verify first
internal reconciliation; no external load-bearing reader found

gaps: traces table has no load-bearing consumer — first to cut/sample (#3069)
circular: collectMentions reads messages_fts then writes back a crawler row, re-indexed, read again (#2323)
monolith spans 4 owners — ownership violation pending decomposition #3069/ADR-033
